one_line_definition: Ratio of drawing length to true length, used to define drawing scale
A one-dimensional ruler scale that reads a single unit directly
A scale that uses diagonal lines to subdivide units into finer increments than a plain scale allows
A pictorial drawing method where all three axes are drawn at equal scales, with horizontals at 30° to the base
A pictorial method where the front face is drawn true shape and receding lines are drawn at an angle (usually 45°)
A multiview drawing method that shows an object from multiple perpendicular directions onto flat planes
Orthographic convention in which each view is projected onto the plane behind the object
Orthographic convention in which each view is projected onto the plane in front of the object
A supplementary orthographic view projected onto a plane inclined to the principal planes, used to show true shape of slanted faces
The condition where a line or arc meets a curve at exactly one point with no crossing, sharing the same tangent direction
A curve produced by the intersection of a plane with a cone; includes the circle, ellipse, parabola, and hyperbola
The set of all points satisfying a given geometric condition; used to trace paths of mechanism linkages
The curve traced by a point on the rim of a circle rolling along a straight line
The curve traced by the end of a taut string unwinding from a cylinder; used for gear tooth profiles
A spiral where the distance between successive turns is constant; r = aθ
A three-dimensional curve advancing uniformly along a cylinder or cone; the basis for screw threads
The curve formed where two solid shapes penetrate each other; found by the cutting-plane method
The 2-D flat shape produced by unfolding the surface of a 3-D solid; used for sheet-metal layout
A figure of different shape but identical area constructed by geometric means (e.g., rectangle equal in area to a triangle)
In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides; used geometrically to combine areas
The British Standards Institution publication specifying engineering drawing practice, including projection, sectioning, dimensioning, and thread representation
A drawing view showing the internal features of an object by cutting it with an imaginary plane and removing the near half
A helical ridge on a cylindrical shaft used for fastening or power transmission; represented conventionally in engineering drawings per BS 308
The annotation of drawings with size and location information, following standardised rules for completeness and clarity
A drawing that shows multiple parts together in their working relationship, typically with part numbers and a parts list
An iron-carbon alloy (>2% C) with high hardness and brittleness; sub-types include gray, ductile, white, and malleable cast iron
Steel with carbon as the primary alloying element (up to ~2%); properties depend strongly on carbon content and heat treatment
A steel alloy with at least 10.5% chromium, providing corrosion resistance through a passive oxide layer
High-carbon, high-alloy steels designed for hardness, wear resistance, and toughness in cutting and forming tools
Alloys designed for service at elevated temperatures and/or extreme mechanical demands, including nickel, cobalt, and iron-based superalloys
Controlled heating and cooling of metals to alter microstructure and mechanical properties (e.g., annealing, quenching, tempering)
